label: Case Based Discussion (ticket)
notes: Turas SLE TICKET for Case Based Discussion — the trainee REQUESTS the SLE; the assessor completes it later, so there are no ratings or assessor-feedback fields here. ASP.NET MVC. The ticket page hosts all four SLE types at once, each behind a SelectedForms_XxxFormTicketed checkbox with its own prefixed fields (CbdTitle, CbdHistory...), so we tick our form's box first and fill only its block. Yes/No radios share one id and differ only by value=True/False — matched by SELECTOR per option, never by id. The Recipient (assessor) list is ACCOUNT-SPECIFIC (real supervisor names + Turas ids), so it is reference-only in v1: the user picks it on the form. Dates are dd/mm/yyyy (the input carries data-val ukdateparser). REFLECTION: auto-filled from July 2026. Claude rewrites what the trainee dictated into polished, professional reflective prose — faithful tidying, NOT generation. Every fact, insight and learning point must trace back to what was actually said; nothing is invented; nothing dictated = empty box. The trainee reviews it on Turas before sending, as always.

fields.label: Ticket a Case Based Discussion
fields.extractionHints: Always Yes — this ticket is for a Case Based Discussion.

fields.label: Date of event
fields.extractionHints: The date the event took place. Accept spoken dates.

fields.label: Title
fields.extractionHints: A short title for the case, e.g. 'Chest pain in a 60-year-old'. NO patient identifiers.

fields.label: Clinical setting
fields.extractionHints: Acute (ED/theatre/admissions), Non-acute (OPD/ward), Community (GP/home visits), or Other.

fields.label: Other clinical setting
fields.extractionHints: Only if the setting is Other.

fields.label: More detail of setting
fields.extractionHints: Optional extra detail about where it happened.

fields.label: Simulated setting?
fields.extractionHints: Was this in a simulated setting? Usually No for a real patient.

fields.label: Telephone/video consultation?
fields.extractionHints: Did this involve a telephone or video consultation? Usually No if face to face.

fields.label: Clinical problem category
fields.extractionHints: Which Foundation capability the case maps to (several allowed): Clinical Assessment, Clinical Prioritisation, Holistic Planning, Communication and Care, Continuity of Care, or Other.

fields.label: Other category
fields.extractionHints: Only if the category is Other.

fields.label: Case history / summary
fields.extractionHints: The account of the case — what happened, what you did. This is the main narrative the assessor will discuss with you. NO patient identifiers (no names, hospital numbers, DOB).

fields.label: Reflection
fields.extractionHints:
Write the trainee's REFLECTION on this case, as a polished, professional piece of reflective writing in the FIRST PERSON — ready to submit.

HOW TO WRITE IT:
• Turn what the trainee said — however scrappy, hesitant or colloquial — into clear, well-structured prose.
• Use professional reflective language and a natural structure: what happened, what you thought or felt, what you learnt, and what you would do differently.
• Fix grammar, remove filler and false starts, and join fragments into proper sentences.
• A few sentences is fine. Length must match what was actually said — do NOT pad.

ABSOLUTE RULE — NOTHING INVENTED:
Every fact, feeling, insight and learning point MUST come from what the trainee actually dictated. You are rewriting their thinking, not supplying it.
• Do NOT add learning points, insights or conclusions they did not express.
• Do NOT add clinical facts, outcomes, complications or figures they did not mention.
• Do NOT invent feelings ('I felt anxious') unless they said so.
• Do NOT bolt on generic reflective filler (human factors, situational awareness, team dynamics, 'this highlighted the importance of...') unless that is genuinely what they told you.
• If they said nothing reflective at all, return an EMPTY string. An empty box is correct; an invented reflection is not.

This text goes into a training record under the trainee's name and is read as their own thinking. Rewrite it well — but only what they said.

fields.label: Assessor (recipient)
fields.extractionHints: Who you're sending the ticket to. The list is specific to your Turas account, so it's shown for reference only — you pick them on the form.

fields.label: Comments to assessor
fields.extractionHints: Any message to the assessor with the ticket.
